metadata.scheme_name: Hari Om Ashram Prerit Senior Scientist Award

raw_data.details: The Hari Om Ashram Prerit Senior Scientist Award was established to commemorate the birth centenary of Pujya Shri Mota, the founder of the Hari Om Ashram, Nadiad. It is awarded to an eminent Indian scientist for their innovative and outstanding contributions to the field of Science and Technology. The award comprises a silver plaque, a citation, and a cash prize of ₹4,00,000.
raw_data.benefits: Award Amount: ₹4,00,000Recognition: Silver Plaque and Citation
raw_data.eligibility: The nominee should be an Indian citizen. The nominee should be a scientist. The nominee's contributions must be in Science and Technology.
raw_data.application_process:
Online
Step 1: The applicant visits the [PRL website](https://www.prl.res.in/prl-eng/) and navigate to the "Opportunities" section.
Step 2: Click on "Awards Administered By PRL" and select scheme "Hari Om Ashram Prerit Senior Scientist Award"
Step 3: The candidate downloads the prescribed format of the application form.
Step 4: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Step 5: Candidate can email their applications in the prescribed format to: DIRECTOR@PRL. RES. IN with the subject line "Nomination for The Hari Om Ashram Prerit Senior Scientist Award".
raw_data.documents_required: Nomination Letter (Head of an academic/research institution, Vice-Chancellors, Deans of universities, Fellows of Indian Academies of Science and Engineering, or past award recipients). Detailed CV of the Nominee. Contribution Statement. List of Publications/Works.

The nominee's contributions must be in Science and Technology. Application ProcessOnlineStep 1: The applicant visits the [PRL website](https://www.prl.res.in/prl-eng/) and navigate to the "Opportunities" section. Step 2: Click on "Awards Administered By PRL" and select scheme "Hari Om Ashram Prerit Senior Scientist Award" Step 3: The candidate downloads the prescribed format of the application form. Step 4: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required). Step 5: Candidate can email their applications in the prescribed format to: DIRECTOR@PRL. RES. IN with the subject line "Nomination for The Hari Om Ashram Prerit Senior Scientist Award". Documents RequiredNomination Letter (Head of an academic/research institution, Vice-Chancellors, Deans of universities, Fellows of Indian Academies of Science and Engineering, or past award recipients). Detailed CV of the Nominee. Contribution Statement. List of Publications/Works. Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the Hari Om Ashram Prerit Senior Scientist Award? It is an award recognizing an eminent Indian scientist for outstanding and innovative contributions in Science and Technology. What is the prize for the award? The award includes a silver plaque, a citation, and a cash prize of ₹4,00,000. Who is eligible for this award? Indian scientists who have made significant contributions in Science and Technology are eligible. Who can nominate a candidate? Heads of academic and research institutions, Vice-Chancellors, Deans of universities, Fellows of Indian Academies of Science and Engineering, and past award recipients can submit nominations.

How should nominations be submitted? Nominations should be sent via email to the Director of PRL at DIRECTOR@PRL. RES. IN.

Can past recipients of this award be re-nominated? No, past recipients cannot be re-nominated.

What types of contributions are considered for this award? Innovative and outstanding contributions in Science and Technology are considered. Is there an age limit for this award? There is no specific age limit for the Hari Om Ashram Prerit Senior Scientist Award.

Can non-resident Indians be nominated? No, the award is intended for scientists who are Indian citizens, but their primary work should be conducted in India.

When will the results be announced? The announcement date will be communicated by the Physical Research Laboratory after the submission deadline.

Sources And References[Guidelines](https://www.prl.res.in/prl-eng/opportunities/aabprl/hoapsrscientistaward)OkWas this helpful?
